div1.entryFree: Undistinguished, not discernible in its peculiar form and nature: as often shrieking u. woe, in clamours of all size, both high and low, Compl. 20. O u. space of woman's wit! (Ff O indistinguished space of woman's will); a plot upon her virtuous husband's life; and the exchange my brother, Lr. IV, 6, 278 (i. e. incalculable, unaccountable; German: unberechenbar. The general interpretation is: boundless, unlimited).
